ops.text:
ORCHESTRATOR RULING on PREP's two check-back gates (PREP was correct to stop; no arms/test run, ~$4 spent, TEST untouched). Load-bearing fact observed in code: the DEPLOYED triage judge default tier is `medium`=sonnet (cc_steer/triage.py:391); the auditor is `large`=opus (:434); the stored auditor-gold judge corpus was generated at large=opus (a stronger reference, per the verdict-corpus convention). So sonnet scoring is faithful to the deployed judge, and the two gates resolve as:

GATE 1 (self-agreement 0.82 < 0.85) — WAIVED. The 0.85 gate assumed a same-model re-run; here it compared re-run SONNET-v6 against the STORED OPUS-v6 corpus, a cross-model contrast it cannot structurally reach. Harness validity is instead established by PREP's own evidence: sonnet-v6-vs-auditor = 0.84 ≥ stored-opus-v6-vs-auditor = 0.82 (sonnet tracks the gold at least as well as the opus corpus). AMENDED harness-validity check going forward: sonnet-v6-vs-auditor ≥ stored-opus-v6-vs-auditor. (This ruling pre-empts the identical latent gate in E33/E35 — same sonnet-scoring, same stored-opus baseline; apply the same waiver there.)

GATE 2 (cost 10-20x over $20) — RESOLVED WITHOUT A CAP RAISE via a lean invocation, because E34's instrument is agreement-vs-auditor-GOLD and its cost metric is a MULTIPLIER (ratio vs single-pass): the ~52k-input-tok/call Claude-Code CLI scaffolding overhead cancels in the paired comparison and is irrelevant to the science — it only gates affordability. AMENDMENT: (a) all passes (baseline + arms, identical) use a LEAN claude CLI invocation — Claude Code tool scaffolding stripped + thinking off via ClaudeConfig — purely to fit budget; the driver's FIRST action confirms the lean invocation is achievable and measures its cost/call, else check-back. (b) Cost reported TWO ways: the sample-count multiplier (science) AND the projected deployed $/1k-triage using the separately-measured SCAFFOLDED cost/call (~$0.083) so synthesis sees the true adoption cost of TTC. (c) Arms TRIMMED per the E30.3 finding that the judge's errors are SYSTEMATIC (over-flags steering 93:12) — plain self-consistency reduces variance, not systematic bias, so DROP SC-7 (redundant variance-reducer); keep SC-3 as a cheap variance control and GenRM-best-of-8 as the arm that can actually correct bias (its verifier can catch over-flagging). (d) $20 hard cap STANDS; driver projects the trimmed+lean design and, if it still busts, applies the registered fallback ladder (cheapest arm beating baseline → dev-only). Re-dispatching now.
